201, created, when you will do that then your post method will return ResponseEntity<Void> which comes to your service layer and it will return void or exception if failed.

I think this should be mediatype as suggested by intellij,
in ncmp 201 returns void, but here it returns string which is valid but out-sync with ncmp. need to discuss it after code freeze. please put this pointer in dmi backlog jira.

It is being used by two http methods
- PUT -> for read
- POST -> for write

Do we expect 'read' and 'create' operation to be supported by both PUT and POST?
why we decided to use same request for both put (read) and post (write)?
nb but important: We should add validation in the controller itself and pass actual object ( MediaType ) to service layer.
nb: We are not mocking with the input arguments and hence not validating that sdnc operation is called with correct input.
testing with the string but in the next test we are testing with Object.
yes, write can be done using post, put , patch and delete.
originally they used separate schemas. I changed this as per previous review comment to combine them.

put can support read, update
post can support read, create
patch -> update
delete -> delete

so rather than writing operation object for each method , i think its better to combine them and throw error in controller if any ops disjunction appear there.
It might be possible that datatype is custom and not supported in Mediatype enum , how would that be handled?
they are distinguished by operation : read or write,update,delete in the request body

We should have correct open api spec but in this case we are adding enums which are never to be supported by each operations and returning an error from implementation. 
We need to discuss it in the daily.

We are converting dataType to MediaType in SDNCOperation class, so, we do have an assumption that it is a supported MediaType enum.

If the intention is different, then we need to revisit the existing implementation.
Had a discussion on this with Toine and Rahul, we have agreed we think its best to combine the schemas as there could be many variations. We can discuss this further in stand up if needed.

We have agreed to remove getting this parameter from the body and just make application/json the default for now until we support more content types. The data type will need to be a string as application/yang-data+json is not a media type and this content type will need to supported in the future
not really need but is OK to pass on (for future use)

If this operation supports write in general and if not limited to creation (as per its description), then success response code should also be general with 200 OK.

Is there any reason to expose sdnc http reponse here ? Does DmiService care about this implementation detail ? It should be only interested in the result of the operation: returns successfully or exception.
 
Then, http response can be kept hidden within SdncOperations, SdncOperation has to deal with its http response and throws a business exception if needed.

In general. the less a component knows about its dependencies, better are the chances to be able to change or refactor these dependencies internally without impacting the components that are using them.
